heaviest_rainfall.status: The storm today is ranked highest for the amount of rainfall recorded during one storm.  47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over 144.8 hours.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 24.6mm per hour was the peak rain rate recorded today ranking 2nd in our overall records.  The storm lasted 144.8 hours and 47.89mm of rain fell.  The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 (ranking first from our records for the longest storm) and it produced a total of 47.89mm of rain at a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm around.  It's the first significant stormy weather event since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: A significant rainy period is on-going and is ranked 8th for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 9 consecutive days beginning 07 November 2025.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is no heatwaves at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 heatwavess, with an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (183°), which differs from the daily average south-westerly (229.0°) by approximately 46.0 degrees.    In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.8°C) and outside temperature (down 1.7°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.2 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (up 0.1 mm) and cloud levels (up 275 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 130.4m higher than yesterday, 24.6m lower than the past week, 274.6m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 561m to a maximum of 715m, averaging 742.0m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 766.6m and 1016.6m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; mild temperatures are expected. Also, pleasant humidity levels and mostly calm with light air movement. Drizzle or light rain possible, fog or low cloud is expected, and moderate levels of sunlight will be present. Weather could become unsettled.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (95%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
